channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Looking back to look ahead: from Deepfakes to DeepSeek what lies ahead in 2025
item.description: The past year was a whirlwind for CIOs and CISOs, marked by the rapid expansion of enterprise AI, persistent cyber threats, and the growing menace of deepfakes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: America’s answer to Concorde completes its first supersonic flight in a California desert
item.description: Boom Supersonic, the American company building what promises to be the world’s fastest airliner, broke the sound barrier for its first time with a test flight in Mojave.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers
item.description: Steve Nulter, a senior business analyst in the Quality Service Management Office, said more agencies are using financial management shared services. The post Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: AMD Radeon Drivers Reveal FSR 4 DLL File: Implications for RDNA 3 and Handheld Gaming PCs
item.description: Unofficial AMD Radeon drivers have revealed the existence of an FSR 4 DLL file, suggesting that RDNA 4 GPUs will be required to access this new upscaling method. This could have significant implications for handheld gaming PCs, particularly those using Ryzen Z2 Extreme APUs with built-in RDNA 3.5 architecture.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The WWE 2K25 release date is here, with Roman Reigns revealed as cover star
item.description: WWE 2K25 has officially been announced with world-renowned wrestler Roman Reigns revealed as the cover star.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Epson's super-bright new ultra short throw 4K projectors promise easy elite home theater pictures up to 160 inches
item.description: Epson's new short-throw laser projectors deliver big, bright 4K images and smart home integration too
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Are Nvidia’s RTX 5060 GPUs about to arrive? Rumor hints they might be - with the RTX 5060 Ti potentially coming with 16GB VRAM
item.description: Nvidia RTX 5060 rumor suggests 8GB of VRAM, with 5060 Ti possibly offering 16GB – a predictable, if disappointing, potential scenario.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: Chinese tech startup DeepSeek’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A distributed state of mind: Event-driven multi-agent systems
item.description: While large language models are useful for chatbots, Q&A systems, translation, and other language tasks, their real power emerges when they can act on insights, automating a broader range of problems. In other words, we unlock their greatest potential when we tap their reasoning capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: UAE leads healthcare revolution with cutting-edge AI and technology
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, the UAE is set to redefine the healthcare landscape, blending cutting-edge technology with medical innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: DOJ indicts North Korean conspirators for remote IT work scheme
item.description: The US Department of Justice this week announced that it had indicted two North Korean nationals and three other men, accusing them of participating in a conspiracy designed to trick US companies into funding the North Korean regime.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
